heading: HUBZone program

Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “A base closure area shall be treated as a HUBZone—Pub. L. 115–91, § 1701(c)(1)“(I) with respect to a census tract or nonmetropolitan county described in clause (i), for a period of not less than 8 years, beginning on the date the military installation undergoes final closure and ending on the date the Administrator makes a final determination as to whether or not to implement the applicable designation described in subparagraph (A) or (B) in accordance with the results of the decennial census conducted after the area was initially designated as a base closure area; and“(II) if such area was treated as a HUBZone at any time after 2010, until such time as the Administrator makes a final determination as to whether or not to implement the applicable designation described in subparagraph (A) or (B), after the 2020 decennial census.”Subsec. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There is established within the Administration a program (to be known as the HUBZone program) to be carried out by the Administrator to provide for Federal contracting assistance, including promoting economic development in economically distressed areas (as defined in section 636(m)(11)), to qualified HUBZone small business concerns in accordance with this section.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: qualified census tracts;

children.label: B
children.text: qualified nonmetropolitan counties;

children.label: C
children.text: lands within the external boundaries of an Indian reservation;

children.label: D
children.text: redesignated areas;

children.label: E
children.text: base closure areas;

children.label: F
children.text: qualified disaster areas; or

children.label: G
children.text: a Governor-designated covered area.

children.label: A
children.text: a small business concern that is at least 51 percent owned and controlled by United States citizens;

children.label: i
children.text: an Alaska Native Corporation owned and controlled by Natives (as determined pursuant to ); orsection 1626(e)(1) of title 43

children.label: ii
children.text: a direct or indirect subsidiary corporation, joint venture, or partnership of an Alaska Native Corporation qualifying pursuant to , if that subsidiary, joint venture, or partnership is owned and controlled by Natives (as determined pursuant to );section 1626(e)(1) of title 43section 1626(e)(2) of title 43

children.label: i
children.text: that is wholly owned by one or more Indian tribal governments, or by a corporation that is wholly owned by one or more Indian tribal governments; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that is owned in part by one or more Indian tribal governments, or by a corporation that is wholly owned by one or more Indian tribal governments, if all other owners are either United States citizens or small business concerns;

children.label: i
children.text: that is wholly owned by one or more Native Hawaiian Organizations (as defined in ), or by a corporation that is wholly owned by one or more Native Hawaiian Organizations; orsection 637(a)(15) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: that is owned in part by one or more Native Hawaiian Organizations, or by a corporation that is wholly owned by one or more Native Hawaiian Organizations, if all other owners are either United States citizens or small business concerns;

children.label: i
children.text: wholly owned by a community development corporation that has received financial assistance under part 1 of subchapter A of the Community Economic Development Act of 1981 ( et seq.); or42 U.S.C. 9805

children.label: ii
children.text: owned in part by one or more community development corporations, if all other owners are either United States citizens or small business concerns; or

children.label: i
children.text: a small agricultural cooperative organized or incorporated in the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: wholly owned by 1 or more small agricultural cooperatives organized or incorporated in the United States; or

children.label: iii
children.text: owned in part by 1 or more small agricultural cooperatives organized or incorporated in the United States, if all owners are small business concerns or United States citizens.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “qualified census tract” means a census tract that is covered by the definition of “qualified census tract” in and that is reflected in an online tool prepared by the Administrator described under subsection (d)(7).section 42(d)(5)(B)(ii) of title 26

children.label: I
children.text: 10 years after the date that the Administrator implements this clause, or

children.label: II
children.text: the date on which the Financial Oversight and Management Board for the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico created by the Puerto Rico Oversight, Management, and Economic Stability Act ceases to exist,

children.label: i
children.text: that was not located in a metropolitan statistical area (as defined in ) at the time of the most recent census taken for purposes of selecting qualified census tracts under ; andsection 143(k)(2)(B) of title 26section 42(d)(5)(B)(ii) of title 26

children.label: I
children.text: the median household income is less than 80 percent of the State median household income, based on a 5-year average of the available data from the Bureau of the Census of the Department of Commerce;

children.label: II
children.text: the unemployment rate is not less than 140 percent of the average unemployment rate for the United States or for the State in which such county is located, whichever is less, based on a 5-year average of the available data from the Secretary of Labor; or

children.label: III
children.text: there is located a difficult development area, as designated by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development in accordance with , within Alaska, Hawaii, or any territory or possession of the United States outside the 48 contiguous States.section 42(d)(5)(B)(iii) of title 26

children.heading: Redesignated area
children.label: C Redesignated area
children.text: The term “redesignated area” means any census tract that ceases to be qualified under subparagraph (A) and any nonmetropolitan county that ceases to be qualified under subparagraph (B) for a period of 3 years after the date on which the census tract or nonmetropolitan county ceased to be so qualified.

children.label: aa
children.text: the Defense Base Closure and Realignment Act of 1990 (part A of title XXIX of division B of ; note);Public Law 101–51010 U.S.C. 2687

children.label: bb
children.text: title II of the Defense Authorization Amendments and Base Closure and Realignment Act (; note);Public Law 100–52610 U.S.C. 2687

children.label: cc
children.text: ; orsection 2687 of title 10

children.label: dd
children.text: any other provision of law authorizing or directing the Secretary of Defense or the Secretary of a military department to dispose of real property at the military installation for purposes relating to base closures of redevelopment, while retaining the authority to enter into a leaseback of all or a portion of the property for military use;

children.label: II
children.text: the census tract or nonmetropolitan county in which the lands described in subclause (I) are wholly contained;

children.label: III
children.text: a census tract or nonmetropolitan county the boundaries of which intersect the area described in subclause (I); and

children.label: IV
children.text: a census tract or nonmetropolitan county the boundaries of which are contiguous to the area described in subclause (II) or subclause (III).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: A census tract or nonmetropolitan county described in clause (i) shall be considered to be a base closure area for a period beginning on the date on which the Administrator designates such census tract or nonmetropolitan county as a base closure area and ending on the date on which the base closure area ceases to be a qualified census tract under subparagraph (A) or a qualified nonmetropolitan county under subparagraph (B) in accordance with the online tool prepared by the Administrator described under subsection (d)(7), except that such period may not be less than 8 years.

children.heading: Census tract
children.label: I Census tract
children.text: The term “census tract” means a census tract delineated by the United States Bureau of the Census in the most recent decennial census that is not located in a nonmetropolitan county and does not otherwise qualify as a qualified census tract.

children.heading: Nonmetropolitan county
children.label: II Nonmetropolitan county
children.text: The term “nonmetropolitan county” means a county that was not located in a metropolitan statistical area (as defined in ) at the time of the most recent census taken for purposes of selecting qualified census tracts and does not otherwise qualify as a qualified nonmetropolitan county.section 143(k)(2)(B) of title 26

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the term “qualified disaster area” means any census tract or nonmetropolitan county located in an area where a major disaster has occurred or an area in which a catastrophic incident has occurred if such census tract or nonmetropolitan county ceased to be qualified under subparagraph (A) or (B), as applicable, during the period beginning 5 years before the date on which the President declared the major disaster or the catastrophic incident occurred.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a major disaster, on the date on which the President declared the major disaster for the area in which the census tract or nonmetropolitan county, as applicable, is located; or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a catastrophic incident, on the date on which the catastrophic incident occurred in the area in which the census tract or nonmetropolitan county, as applicable, is located.

children.heading: Major disaster
children.label: I Major disaster
children.text: The term “major disaster” means a major disaster declared by the President under .section 5170 of title 42

children.heading: Other definitions
children.label: II Other definitions
children.text: The terms “census tract” and “nonmetropolitan county” have the meanings given such terms in subparagraph (D)(iii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A “Governor-designated covered area” means a covered area that the Administrator has designated by approving a petition described under clause (ii).

children.label: I
children.text: the potential for job creation and investment in the covered area;

children.label: II
children.text: the demonstrated interest of small business concerns in the covered area to be designated as a Governor-designated covered area;

children.label: III
children.text: how State and local government officials have incorporated the covered area into an economic development strategy; and

children.label: IV
children.text: if the covered area was a HUBZone before becoming the subject of the petition, the impact on the covered area if the Administrator did not approve the petition.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: iii Limitations
children.text: Each calendar year, a Governor may submit not more than 1 petition described under clause (ii). Such petition shall include all covered areas in a State for which the Governor seeks designation as a Governor-designated covered area, except that the total number of covered areas included in such petition may not exceed 10 percent of the total number of covered areas in the State.

children.heading: Certification
children.label: iv Certification
children.text: If the Administrator grants a petition described under clause (ii), the Governor of the Governor-designated covered area shall, not less frequently than annually, submit data to the Administrator certifying that each Governor-designated covered area continues to meet the requirements of clause (v)(I).

children.label: aa
children.text: that is located outside of an urbanized area, as determined by the Bureau of the Census;

children.label: bb
children.text: with a population of not more than 50,000; and

children.label: cc
children.text: for which the average unemployment rate is not less than 120 percent of the average unemployment rate of the United States or of the State in which the covered area is located, whichever is less, based on the most recent data available from the American Community Survey conducted by the Bureau of the Census.

children.heading: Governor
children.label: II Governor
children.text: The term “Governor” means the chief executive of a State.

children.heading: State
children.label: III State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa.

children.heading: Qualified HUBZone small business concern
children.label: 4 Qualified HUBZone small business concern
children.text: The term “qualified HUBZone small business concern” means a HUBZone small business concern that has been certified by the Administrator in accordance with the procedures described in this section.

children.heading: Alaska Native Corporation
children.label: A Alaska Native Corporation
children.text: The term “Alaska Native Corporation” has the same meaning as the term “Native Corporation” in .section 1602 of title 43

children.heading: Alaska Native Village
children.label: B Alaska Native Village
children.text: The term “Alaska Native Village” has the same meaning as the term “Native village” in .section 1602 of title 43

children.label: I
children.text: any lands that are located within a State in which a tribe did not exercise governmental jurisdiction on , unless that tribe is recognized after , by either an Act of Congress or pursuant to regulations of the Secretary of the Interior for the administrative recognition that an Indian group exists as an Indian tribe (part 83 of title 25, Code of Federal Regulations); andDecember 21, 2000December 21, 2000

children.label: II
children.text: lands taken into trust or acquired by an Indian tribe after , if such lands are not located within the external boundaries of an Indian reservation or former reservation or are not contiguous to the lands held in trust or restricted status on ; andDecember 21, 2000December 21, 2000

children.label: I
children.text: are within the jurisdictional areas of an Oklahoma Indian tribe (as determined by the Secretary of the Interior); and

children.label: II
children.text: are recognized by the Secretary of the Interior as eligible for trust land status under part 151 of title 25, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ).December 21, 2000

children.heading: Agricultural commodity
children.label: 6 Agricultural commodity
children.text: The term “agricultural commodity” has the same meaning as in .section 5602 of title 7

children.label: A
children.text: the term “contracting officer” has the meaning given that term in ; andsection 2101(1) of title 41

children.label: B
children.text: the term “full and open competition” has the meaning given that term in .section 107 of title 41

children.label: i
children.text: the qualified HUBZone small business concern is determined to be a responsible contractor with respect to performance of such contract opportunity, and the contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that 2 or more qualified HUBZone small business concerns will submit offers for the contracting opportunity;

children.label: I
children.text: $7,000,000, in the case of a contract opportunity assigned a standard industrial classification code for manufacturing; or

children.label: II
children.text: $3,000,000, in the case of all other contract opportunities; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the estimation of the contracting officer, the contract award can be made at a fair and reasonable price.

children.heading: Restricted competition
children.label: B Restricted competition
children.text: A contract opportunity may be awarded pursuant to this section on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small business concerns if the contracting officer has a reasonable expectation that not less than 2 qualified HUBZone small business concerns will submit offers and that the award can be made at a fair market price.

children.heading: Appeals
children.label: C Appeals
children.text: Not later than 5 days from the date the Administration is notified of a procurement officer’s decision not to award a contract opportunity under this section to a qualified HUBZone small business concern, the Administrator may notify the contracting officer of the intent to appeal the contracting officer’s decision, and within 15 days of such date the Administrator may file a written request for reconsideration of the contracting officer’s decision with the Secretary of the department or agency head.

children.heading: Price evaluation preference in full and open competitions
children.label: 3 Price evaluation preference in full and open competitions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), in any case in which a contract is to be awarded on the basis of full and open competition, the price offered by a qualified HUBZone small business concern shall be deemed as being lower than the price offered by another offeror (other than another small business concern), if the price offered by the qualified HUBZone small business concern is not more than 10 percent higher than the price offered by the otherwise lowest, responsive, and responsible offeror.

children.label: i
children.text: 10 percent, for the portion of a contract to be awarded that is not greater than 25 percent of the total volume being procured for each commodity in a single invitation;

children.label: ii
children.text: 5 percent, for the portion of a contract to be awarded that is greater than 25 percent, but not greater than 40 percent, of the total volume being procured for each commodity in a single invitation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: zero, for the portion of a contract to be awarded that is greater than 40 percent of the total volume being procured for each commodity in a single invitation.

children.heading: Procurement of commodities for international food aid export operations
children.label: C Procurement of commodities for international food aid export operations
children.text: The price evaluation preference for purchases of agricultural commodities by the Secretary of Agriculture for export operations through international food aid programs administered by the Farm Service Agency shall be 5 percent on the first portion of a contract to be awarded that is not greater than 20 percent of the total volume of each commodity being procured in a single invitation.

children.heading: Treatment of preference
children.label: D Treatment of preference
children.text: A contract awarded to a HUBZone small business concern under a preference described in subparagraph (B) shall not be counted toward the fulfillment of any requirement partially set aside for competition restricted to small business concerns.

children.heading: Relationship to other contracting preferences
children.label: 4 Relationship to other contracting preferences
children.text: A procurement may not be made from a source on the basis of a preference provided in paragraph (2) or (3), if the procurement would otherwise be made from a different source under section 4124 or 4125 of title 18 or chapter 85 of title 41.

children.label: A
children.text: at the time of certification and at each examination conducted pursuant to paragraph (4), the principal office of the concern is located in a HUBZone and not fewer than 35 percent of its employees reside in a HUBZone;

children.label: B
children.text: the concern will attempt to maintain the applicable employment percentage under subparagraph (A) during the performance of any contract awarded to such concern on the basis of a preference provided under subsection (c); and

children.label: C
children.text: the concern will ensure that the requirements of are satisfied with respect to any subcontract entered into by such concern pursuant to a contract awarded under this section.section 657s of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the filing, investigation, and disposition by the Administration of any challenge to the eligibility of a HUBZone small business concern to receive assistance under this section (including a challenge, filed by an interested party, relating to the veracity of documentation provided to the Administration by such a concern under paragraph (1)); and

children.label: B
children.text: verification by the Administrator of the accuracy of any documentation provided by a HUBZone small business concern under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Timing
children.label: 3 Timing
children.text: The Administrator shall verify the eligibility of a HUBZone small business concern using the procedures described in paragraph (2) within a reasonable time and not later than 60 days after the date on which the Administrator receives sufficient and complete documentation from a HUBZone small business concern under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Recertification
children.label: 4 Recertification
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the date that such HUBZone small business concern was certified as a qualified HUBZone small business concern, and every 3 years thereafter, the Administrator shall verify the accuracy of any documentation provided by a HUBZone small business concern under paragraph (1) to determine if such HUBZone small business concern remains a qualified HUBZone small business concern.

children.heading: Examinations
children.label: 5 Examinations
children.text: The Administrator shall conduct program examinations of qualified HUBZone small business concerns, using a risk-based analysis to select which concerns are examined, to ensure that any concern examined meets the requirements of paragraph (1).

children.heading: Loss of certification
children.label: 6 Loss of certification
children.text: A HUBZone small business concern that, based on the results of an examination conducted pursuant to paragraph (5) no longer meets the requirements of paragraph (1), shall have 30 days to submit documentation to the Administrator to be eligible to be certified as a qualified HUBZone small business concern. During the 30-day period, such concern may not compete for or be awarded a contract under this section. If such concern fails to meet the requirements of paragraph (1) by the last day of the 30-day period, the Administrator shall not certify such concern as a qualified HUBZone small business concern.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to HUBZones described under subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (b)(3), beginning on , and every 5 years thereafter;January 1, 2020

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to a HUBZone described under subsection (b)(3)(C), immediately after the area becomes, or ceases to be, a redesignated area; and

children.label: iii
children.text: with respect to HUBZones described under subparagraphs (D), (E), and (F) of subsection (b)(3), immediately after an area is designated as a base closure area, qualified disaster area, or Governor-designated covered area, respectively.

children.heading: Data
children.label: B Data
children.text: The online tool required under subparagraph (A) shall clearly and conspicuously provide access to the data used by the Administrator to determine whether or not an area is a HUBZone in the year in which the online tool was prepared.

children.heading: Notification of update
children.label: C Notification of update
children.text: The Administrator shall include in the online tool a notification of the date on which the online tool, and the data used to create the online tool, will be updated.

children.heading: List of qualified HUBZone small business concerns
children.label: 8 List of qualified HUBZone small business concerns
A
children.text: to the extent practicable, include the name, address, and type of business with respect to such concern;

children.label: B
children.text: be updated by the Administrator not less than annually; and

children.label: C
children.text: be provided upon request to any Federal agency or other entity.

children.heading: Provision of data
children.label: 9 Provision of data
children.text: Upon the request of the Administrator, the Secretary of Labor, the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, and the Secretary of the Interior (or the Assistant Secretary for Indian Affairs), shall promptly provide to the Administrator such information as the Administrator determines to be necessary to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 10 Penalties
children.text: In addition to the penalties described in , any small business concern that is determined by the Administrator to have misrepresented the status of that concern as a “qualified HUBZone small business concern” for purposes of this section shall be subject to liability for fraud, including and sections 3729 through 3733 of title 31.section 645(d) of this titlesection 1001 of title 18

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Administrator shall publish performance metrics designed to measure the success of the HUBZone program established under this section in meeting the program’s objective of promoting economic development in economically distressed areas (as defined in ).December 12, 2017section 636(m)(11) of this title

children.heading: Collecting and managing HUBZone data
children.label: 2 Collecting and managing HUBZone data
children.text: The Administrator shall develop processes to incentivize each regional office of the Administration to collect and manage data on HUBZones within the geographic area served by such regional office.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of HUBZone small business concerns that lost certification as a qualified HUBZone small business concern because of the results of an examination performed under subsection (d)(5); and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of those concerns that did not submit documentation to be recertified under subsection (d)(6).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out the program established by this section $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2020 through 2025.
